TextENGNew: This piece holds a very important meaning for us.
Because for the first time, it showed us the possibility of punching a hole in the fate of June 1983 — that fate we had accepted as utterly inescapable.
changeReason: New translation adds 'that fate we had accepted as utterly inescapable', capturing the Japanese 抗えない運命と諦めていた (the resignation to an irresistible fate) which the original omitted entirely.

TextENGNew: To state it plainly: in the end, everything is swallowed by Rule Y and undone.
And yet, it confronts Rule X — the great law that governs this board — head-on, and proves, drawing on all that was learned from the pieces that came before, that it can be defeated.
changeReason: Old translation uses present tense 'we're challenging' with 'we' as subject, but the Japanese uses past tense (挑み...証明した) with the piece/fragment as the implicit subject; new translation corrects both the tense and perspective.

TextENGNew: Even in a game where victory seemed impossible, we can find a sliver of hope.
That is what this precious piece has taught us...
changeReason: New translation adds 'precious' (とても大切な) and the trailing ellipsis, both present in the Japanese; the original omitted the emotional qualifier entirely.

TextENGNew: Within this piece, Rule X is all but completely shattered.
Keiichi Maebara and the others will never again be led astray by something so pathetic as Hinamizawa Syndrome, never again made to dance in tragedy...
changeReason: Old translation uses 'any longer' and adds 'our other friends' not present in the Japanese; new translation uses 'never again' matching the finality of 二度とない and removes the invented 'friends' qualifier.

TextENGNew: It has also taught us that the Rules are not invincible — that they can be broken.
In other words, it has given hope to us — those watching from outside the game board.
...A crucial piece — the origin of everything, and the turning point that changes it all...
